Average generation time to use in metric calculations (i.e. for generational average calculated as geometric mean). Can be any integer; but typical values are 3 to 5 depending on species and area. Note that pink salmon are generally age 2 returns in either even or odd years; so the AvgGen used for metric calculations is axctually set to 1 (just use the annual value).
Is the dominant age class highly stable or does change each year? Possible values are High (define); Moderate (define); Low (define); or None (define); or Unknown.
Is there evidence of persistent change in dominant age? Possible values are Strong (define); Moderate (define); Weak(define); None (define); or Unknown.
If age composition is variable or changing; include a brief note describing how the value for AvgGen was selected.

Group of CUs used for assessing major fish stocks under the Fish Stock Provisions of the Fisheries Act (e.g.; in an FSAR/FSRR process). These are legally defined grouping with associated requirements for science advice and management response.

Group of CUs assessed together in a WSP status assessment process; based on various criteria (e.g.; data processing done together).  Examples are CK-Inner_South_Coast; CK-Fraser_Lower; SK-Fraser_Esum; CO-Fraser_Int. These are practical groupings for operational purposes (e.g.; planning routine status updates).
